title: Calonyction album
intro: Calonyction album, also known as Queen's Wreath or Moonflower, is a perennial climbing vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating fever, due to its antioxidant, antispasmodic, and antipyretic effects. The bioactive constituents of Calonyction album include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its pharmacological properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calonyction album include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory issues to skin conditions.
